title: Fix ENI exhaustion with VPC CNI prefix delegation
scenario: New pods are stuck Pending with `failed to allocate for range 0: no IP addresses available`. Your m5.large nodes support 3 ENIs × 10 IPs = 29 pod IPs minus the primary IP = 28 usable IPs per node. With WARM_ENI_TARGET=1 you're wasting IPs on warm slots. Enable prefix delegation (`/28` prefixes per ENI) to scale pod density by 16x without adding nodes.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: amazon-vpc-cni
  namespace: kube-system
data:
  WARM_ENI_TARGET: "1"
  MINIMUM_IP_TARGET: "1"
  ENABLE_PREFIX_DELEGATION: "false"

objectives.description: `MINIMUM_IP_TARGET` is set to a non-zero value

hints.text: Prefix delegation assigns /28 CIDR blocks (16 IPs) per ENI slot instead of individual IPs. Enable it with `ENABLE_PREFIX_DELEGATION: "true"`.
Set `WARM_ENI_TARGET: "0"` to stop pre-warming entire ENIs (wasted IPs). Use `MINIMUM_IP_TARGET: "10"` to keep a warm pool of individual IPs instead.
Three changes: `ENABLE_PREFIX_DELEGATION: "true"`, `WARM_ENI_TARGET: "0"`, `MINIMUM_IP_TARGET: "10"`. After applying, the aws-node DaemonSet pods need to restart to pick up the changes.

solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: amazon-vpc-cni
  namespace: kube-system
data:
  WARM_ENI_TARGET: "0"
  MINIMUM_IP_TARGET: "10"
  ENABLE_PREFIX_DELEGATION: "true"
